text: we present a case of combined endolaparoscopic surgery for colon polyp. This is a forty-two year old patient who underwent alloparoscopic assisted vaginal hysterectomy in twenty thirteen. Subsequently, in twenty seventeen, alloparoscopic appendectomy with the result of mucinous cystadenoma. A CT colonography was performed in which a one centimeter polyp was seen in the cecum with a very redundant and folded sigmoid colon in the pelvis as well as unassociated diverticulitis. After two colonoscopies, progression to the left colon was impossible due to the presence of a fixed sigmoid loop. Patient is referred for laparoscopic ileocythectomy.

The video displays axial CT colonography images showing a one-centimeter polyp, along with volume-rendered images. Adhesions of the sigmoid colon to the abdominal wall in the right iliac fossa and pelvis are visible.
The surgeon completes the mobilization of the sigmoid colon after dividing its attachments to the lateral abdominal wall and pelvis, allowing for improved access and manipulation.

The colonoscope is introduced into the sigmoid colon, with laparoscopic clamps assisting its advancement. The scope progresses visually through the ascending colon and posteriorly navigates the splenic flexure towards the transverse colon.
The endoscopic view shows the cecum, where a polyp is identified on the lateral wall. The polyp appears as a submucosal nodule with preserved mucosa.

Adrenaline mixed with Mechelin Blue is injected submucosally, highlighting the depth of the scar and precisely delineating the edges. The polypectomy loop is then inserted, and the tumor is excised with assistance from laparoscopic compression of the intestinal wall.
The surgeon places approximation clips on the edges of the intestinal wall after confirming no perforation. The specimen is then retrieved using a basket rod net.
The endoscopically resected specimen is shown, displaying signs of foreign body reaction, necrosis, and calcification following the surgical procedure.
